acceptance_criteria.text: The report generator identifies the current fixed `Finale` behavior in `4_WINDOW_AGGREGATES` as a template formula problem.

proof.summary: Template inspection found fixed `Finale` formulas like `D21:D23`.

acceptance_criteria.text: Generated workbooks compute the `Finale` row from all segment/frame rows that overlap the last 3 seconds of the video, using `tribe_segments.tsv` timing (`start`/`offset`/`timeline` plus `duration`) when available.

proof.summary: Generated `C8` formula uses rows `D7:D8` for final `[7, 10)` seconds in a 10-second synthetic video.

acceptance_criteria.text: The number of rows used by `Finale` changes with segment timing and is not hard-coded to rows 21-23, frames 18-20, or the last three rows when timing exists.

proof.summary: With timing, selected rows are `(7, 8)`, not fixed rows 21-23 or the fallback `(6, 8)`.

acceptance_criteria.text: If segment timing is unavailable, generation falls back to the previous safe behavior of using up to the last three available rows.

proof.summary: Generated fallback `C8` formula uses `D6:D8` for five rows.

acceptance_criteria.text: The workbook labels for the `Finale` row no longer claim a fixed `18-20с` window.
Focused checks prove the generated formulas and workbook JSON/code syntax are valid.

commands_for_fresh_verifier: Generate a synthetic workbook with 5 two-second segments and inspect `4_WINDOW_AGGREGATES!C8`.
Generate a synthetic workbook without `segments.tsv` and inspect `4_WINDOW_AGGREGATES!C8`.
known_gaps: Excel formula recalculation itself was not evaluated in desktop Excel/LibreOffice; formulas were inspected structurally.
